wk68_g0_diagnose.confirmed_root_cause: H3 (hit-rect coordinate space) -- NOT H1 pause-guard, NOT H2 masked exception.
diagnosis: The building panel renders ALL content into a tall scratch sub-surface (panel_surf, scratch_h up to 2400px), then blits only a CLIPPED, scroll-offset slice into the left-column viewport Rect(panel_x, panel_y, panel_width, viewport_h) with src_y=menu_scroll_px (building_panel.py:333-341).

But every interactive rect is stored at its UN-scrolled scratch position: enter_building_button_rect = Rect(panel_x+local.x, panel_y+local.y, ...) with NO menu_scroll_px term (building_panel.py:502-507); same for demolish (:461-466) and research/blacksmith/library/upgrade/build_catalog in the 3 building_renderers modules. menu_scroll_px appeared NOWHERE in rect computation.

Consequence: when panel content overflows the left-column viewport (hero pinned / 720p / 4-occupant guild), the bottom buttons (Enter renders last/lowest) fall BELOW the clip and are unclickable; scrolling does not help because the stored rect stays frozen at its scratch y while the drawn button moves with src_y.

Evidence lines: building_panel.py:502-507 (enter rect, no scroll term), :461-466 (demolish), :333-341 (clipped scroll-blit src_y=menu_scroll_px), :136-140 (outer bounds-gate). Same pattern in building_renderers/{economic,special,castle}_panel.py. Close (X) at top is unaffected (top-pinned).
ranked_hypotheses_ruled_out: H1 (input_handler.py:421-427 pause-guard) -- left in place; the click reaches building_panel.handle_click fine when the panel is visible. NOT the cause (separate latent issue; Agent 11 owns the paused-click regression test per PM brief).
H2 (try/except at input_handler.py:445) -- no exception fires in the click ladder; the click is simply rejected by the frozen-rect/viewport mismatch. NOT the cause.
wk68_g0_diagnose.reported_to_pm: Confirmed cause H3 + exact lines reported; PM APPROVED root cause and the option-1 + viewport-clamp fix for G1.
